name: Castillo de Cañatravos y Castelserás - return from Alcañiz
name_original: Castillo de Cañatravos y Castelserás - vuelta desde Alcañiz
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Alcañiz traverse a diverse landscape in the Aragon region of Spain, featuring gently rolling hills, agricultural valleys with olive groves, and scrubland. The terrain offers a mix of well-paved surfaces suitable for various fitness levels and more demanding routes with significant elevation gains. Cyclists can explore areas characterized by agricultural fields, river valleys, and historic landmarks. The region provides varied conditions for road cycling, from easier paths to challenging climbs. Best road cycling routes around Alcañiz The most popular road cycling route is Castillo de Cañatravos y Castelserás - return from Alcañiz, a 26.9 miles (43.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a mix of agricultural landscapes and historic sites. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alcañiz to Caspe Loop, a moderate 47.9 miles (77.2 km) path. This route offers diverse landscapes and passes through historic areas of Lower Aragon. Local road cyclists also love the Alcañiz – Valderrobres Stone Bridge loop from Alcañiz, a 62.1 miles (100.0 km) trail leading through varied terrains and towards the town of Valderrobres, often completed in about 4 hours 55 minutes. Road cycling around Alcañiz is defined by rolling hills, agricultural valleys, and historic landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult, longer routes. The routes in Alcañiz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 350 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcañiz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Alcañiz?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes around Alcañiz, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, from agricultural valleys to historic sites.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Alcañiz?
A: Road cycling in Alcañiz offers diverse terrain, including gently rolling hills through agricultural areas with olive groves and scrubland. You'll find well-paved surfaces suitable for various fitness levels, as well as more demanding routes with significant elevation gains, particularly in areas like Lower Aragon. The Val de Zafán Greenway provides a gentler, more accessible experience.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Alcañiz?
A: Yes, Alcañiz offers several routes suitable for beginners and families. The Val de Zafán Greenway, for instance, is known for its gentle slopes and accessibility. Overall, there are 5 easy routes available, providing a good starting point for less experienced cyclists.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see while cycling near Alcañiz?
A: While cycling near Alcañiz, you can encounter various historical and natural landmarks. The region features prehistoric &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5226851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock Art Paintings Near the Guadalope River&lt;/a&gt;, designated as a UNESCO World Heritage site. You might also pass by the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2752464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Equinox Tunnel&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3560752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Railway Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;. The town of Alcañiz itself is overlooked by the 12th-century Castle of Los Calatravos, and its historic center boasts the medieval Plaza de España and the collegiate church of Santa María la Mayor.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Alcañiz offers challenging routes for experienced road cyclists. There are 10 difficult routes with significant elevation gains, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcañiz – Valderrobres Stone Bridge loop from Alcañiz&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 100 km and features over 1400 meters of ascent. These routes provide a good test for fitness and endurance.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alcañiz?
A: The road cycling routes around Alcañiz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, which include agricultural valleys and historic sites, and the variety of routes catering to different ability levels.

Q: Can I find loop routes for road cycling around Alcañiz?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Alcañiz are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958798560/alcaniz-to-caspe-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcañiz to Caspe Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Calatravos – Castelserás loop from Alcañiz&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied distances and experiences.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alcañiz?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling in Alcañiz, offering pleasant temperatures. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are often suitable for riding. Winter can be cooler, but many routes remain accessible.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling options from Alcañiz?
A: For long-distance enthusiasts, the Val de Zafán Greenway is a significant option. While not exclusively a road cycling route, it offers an 180-kilometer path that can be integrated into longer tours, showcasing contrasting scenery from South Aragon towards the sea. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcañiz – Valderrobres Stone Bridge loop from Alcañiz&lt;/a&gt; is also a substantial 100 km route.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Alcañiz?
A: Alcañiz, as a town, provides various parking options. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check local signage or use navigation apps. Many routes start directly from the town, making central parking convenient for accessing the cycling network.

Q: Are there any specific cultural or historical sites worth visiting in Alcañiz after a ride?
A: Absolutely. After a ride, you can explore Alcañiz's rich history. The 12th-century Castle of Los Calatravos, now a parador hotel, offers panoramic views. The historic center features the medieval Plaza de España, with its Renaissance town hall and the impressive Baroque facade of the collegiate church of Santa María la Mayor. The nearby MotorLand Aragón circuit also draws visitors.
tips: The Motorland circuit is one of the most well-known circuits on the international scene, creating a rich population from an area with less economic activity thanks to the circuit.

Castelserás is a town that is part of Baja Aragón, eight km from Alcañiz. The entire town is located on a hill and where the main street separates the old medieval center. At the top is the parish church of Our Lady of the Nativity.
One of the best-known festivals is on January 19, where a large bonfire is made to honor San Sebastian.

The Calatravos castle is today a national tourist stop. Its oldest part dates back to the 13th century, although the cloister and palace date from the 14th century. The outer wall was renovated in the 19th century.
